system: Leonoria Magic Weapons — Wands, Staves, and Foci
note: Magic weapons prioritise spell amplification over physical damage. Physical damage output is intentionally low. The real differentiation is: Control vs Power, Stability vs Volatility, Single-target vs Area casting. Magical properties only activate for the relevant spellcaster class — other classes can use the physical damage but gain no magical benefits.
note_staff_overlap: Magic staves (WM2_xxx) are distinct from combat staves (W2H_023-026). Both deal similar physical blunt damage but magic staves carry spell amplification properties that combat staves do not.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Wand

spell_amplification.description: Grants +10–15 extra Materium Connection points for the duration of combat.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The basic conduit. Focused spellcasting at minimal weight. What it lacks in raw power it makes up for in precision.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Rod

spell_amplification.description: Reduces the chance of spell miscast or overload by 15%. Stronger than a wand in melee, more stable for sustained casting.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Sturdier and shorter than a wand. Less precise, but more resilient to the punishment of a full battle.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Magic Scepter

spell_amplification.description: On equip, grants one random Lightwielding perk (rolled at item generation). Examples: bonus Purelight damage, Solacium range increase, Verdicium scaling improvement.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Ornate and symbolic. The magical properties only express themselves through Lightwielding school casting.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Focus Orb

spell_amplification.description: Enhances spell accuracy and targeting radius. Single-target spells gain +10% hit chance. Area spells gain tighter radius control.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Held or floating at palm level. No weapon — entirely a focusing lens for the mind. Useless if someone walks up close.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Spell Dagger

spell_amplification.description: Any successful melee hit with this weapon applies a random affliction or minor Venomous damage effect on the target in addition to physical damage.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: A ritual blade used as much to direct intent as to cut. The edge carries magic the same way a wick carries fire.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Rune Wand

spell_amplification.description: Inscribed with runes attuned to one specific Materium school (determined at generation). Spells of that school deal 10–20% bonus damage.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The runes are not decoration. Each one is a narrow channel that focuses one specific school very precisely.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Crystal Wand

spell_amplification.description: Embedded resonant crystal improves all spell damage output by 5–15%.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The crystal amplifies. What school of magic is irrelevant — it simply makes everything hit harder.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Bone Wand

spell_amplification.description: Enhances Shadow Arts and primal Materium school spells. Does not interact with Lightwielding spells at all.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Made from the bones of something magical. It still carries that energy, now directed rather than wild.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Branch Focus

spell_amplification.description: Living wood synergises with nature-based and life-school magic. Athropium, Organia, and Lifewhispering spells deal 5–15% bonus damage.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: A living branch, cut and shaped while still green. The life in it responds to nature magic as iron responds to a magnet.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Chain Focus

spell_amplification.description: Rhythmic casting with this focus increases the critical hit chance of spells by 10%.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The rattling rhythm of the chain marks cast time. Practitioners develop a cadence. The magic follows the beat.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Glyph Baton

spell_amplification.description: Drawing spells in the air through this baton reduces the Materium Connection pool cost of all spells by 10%. Improves casting control and shaping.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The glyphs drawn with this direct the spell's shape before it is released. Efficient in a way raw casting never is.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Spirit Totem

spell_amplification.description: Channelling ancestral spirits through this totem improves the wielder's magical resistances by 5–25% (rolled at generation).

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: More fetish than weapon. The spirits invoked through it do not fight so much as protect.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Charmblade

spell_amplification.description: On a successful hit, 20% chance to cause the target to miss their next attack. Also grants +5 Conviction to the wielder in combat.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Short blade threaded with magic. Designed for a caster who cannot always keep their distance. The charm is a deterrent. The blade is a last resort.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Spellbook (Grimoire)

spell_amplification.description: Casting spells through the written incantations in this grimoire amplifies all spell damage output by 20%.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Held open, one hand occupied. Every spell in it was written once and is now read on command. The words know what they are.

one_handed_magic_weapons.name: Lantern Focus

spell_amplification.description: The magical light it emits enhances detection and illumination spells. Also improves the wielder's ability to spot stealthed or hidden enemies by 50%.

one_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: It does not just light the room. It lights what the room is hiding.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Staff (Magic)

spell_amplification.description: Classic long focus. Increases the critical hit chance of all spells by 5–15%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The long staff. Two hands, full leverage, full range. What it lacks in subtlety it supplies in reach and weight.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Greatstaff

spell_amplification.description: Larger and heavier. Higher amplification than a standard staff but slower handling. Increases critical spell chance by 10–20%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Heavy enough to use as a weapon in earnest. The size amplifies both the physical and magical output.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: War Staff

spell_amplification.description: Reinforced for melee use. Blocking with this staff amplifies physical resistance by 10% for the wielder.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: A caster's staff that can actually take a hit. Reinforced where others would splinter. For those who expect to end up in reach.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Crystal Staff

spell_amplification.description: Core crystal acts as a powerful amplifier for all spell output. Improves all spell damage by 5–15%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The crystal at its core catches and magnifies. Magic through it hits harder than through iron or wood.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Totem Staff

spell_amplification.description: Decorated with relics and spirit anchors. Improves all the wielder's magical and physical resistances by 5–15%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The relics on it are not trophies. They are anchors for the spirits that now protect the wielder.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Runic Staff

spell_amplification.description: Covered in structured runes. Enhances one specific Materium school (determined at generation) by 10–20% damage.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Every glyph on the shaft narrows the channel. Less flexible, far more powerful in its chosen direction.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Living Staff

spell_amplification.description: Grown rather than crafted. Adapts to the wielder over time. Improves all damage from life- and nature-related schools by 10–30%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: It is still alive. The leaves that grow from its joints change with the wielder's mood. Over time, it becomes something personal.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Bone Staff

spell_amplification.description: Built from the bones of large magical creatures. Amplifies Shadow Arts and primal Materium school magic. Completely incompatible with Lightwielding.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The bones know what they were. The magic in them never entirely left.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Elemental Staff

spell_amplification.description: Attuned to a single element (determined at generation). Boosts that element's damage from spells by 10%. Increases critical strike chance for that element's damage from spells by 10%.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Narrow and volatile. It amplifies one element extremely well. Everything else is irrelevant to it.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Dragon Totem

spell_amplification.description: Allows the wielder to cast spells even when they are inside an enemy's Zone of Control — normally this would impose severe penalties or prevent casting.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: The totem carries the presence of something vast and unconcerned. Enemies in reach still give it space.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Banner Staff

spell_amplification.description: The magical sigils or banners on this staff bolster allies within 3 hexes against Conviction-based attacks and effects.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Raised in battle, it changes the air around it. Allies near it find it harder to be frightened.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Relic Staff

spell_amplification.description: Ancient artifact as focus. 10% chance on any single-target spell to expand its effect radius by 1 hex, hitting all targets in range.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Old. The magic it contains does not know it should be diminished by time. It isn't.

two_handed_magic_weapons.name: Dual-Core Staff

spell_amplification.description: Contains two active magical cores. 10% of all turns, the wielder gains a second action — this may be a move, a spell, or a physical attack.

two_handed_magic_weapons.flavor: Two cores, each containing a spell's worth of energy. On rare turns the two fire in synchrony, and the wielder gets a moment that should not exist.
